title: Phase 2 completion: verify exit criteria, README, handoff, close epic
description: Verify DESIGN.md Phase 2 exit criteria with evidence: entry loggable in under 5 seconds (quick-log path, manual timing on device); full CRUD verified on Android (flutter run -d android) and web (flutter run -d chrome + DB_SMOKE probe still OK); iOS gate = project still compiles. Run flutter analyze + flutter test --timeout 30s. Update README.md phase table/features (REQUIRED exit step per CLAUDE.md). Write designs/PHASE_2_HANDOFF.md from template, delete PHASE_2_CURRENT_STATUS.md, bd close children + epic, open PR from phase_2 branch.
acceptance_criteria: All exit criteria evidenced; README updated; handoff committed; epic closed; PR opened
close_reason: All exit criteria verified: 69 tests pass, analyze clean, web release build, README updated, handoff written

title: FAB + long-press quick-log type picker
description: Home FAB: tap opens the add sheet (dp-0ms.3). Long-press opens a compact popup with the 7 Bristol type icons (user decision 2026-07-16: type-picker popup, not repeat-last); tapping an icon INSTANTLY saves an entry at occurredAt=now with only that type (no sheet), with a confirmation SnackBar. This is the <5s logging fast path from DESIGN.md. Widget tests for both gestures.
acceptance_criteria: FAB tap opens sheet; long-press picker logs an entry in one tap; widget tests green; analyze clean
close_reason: 3 widget tests green, 69 total pass, analyze clean (commit 864d741)

title: Swipe actions: edit (prefilled sheet) + delete with Undo snackbar
description: Timeline tiles get swipe actions: swipe-to-edit opens the dp-0ms.3 sheet prefilled; swipe-to-delete soft-deletes IMMEDIATELY and shows a SnackBar with Undo (user decision 2026-07-16 — no confirmation dialog). Undo restores by keeping the pre-delete entity in memory and calling repository update(entity) — its .replace() writes all columns which clears deletedAt (verified in drift_bowel_movement_repository.dart). Widget tests: delete removes tile, undo restores it, tombstone respected.
acceptance_criteria: Swipe edit opens prefilled sheet; swipe delete soft-deletes with working Undo; widget tests green; analyze clean

title: Add/Edit entry bottom sheet (Huckleberry pattern)
description: Modal bottom sheet form in lib/features/home/ (or lib/features/log_entry/): BristolTypeSelector (lib/ui/widgets/bristol_type_selector.dart) as primary selector; date + time pickers defaulting to now; optional detail rows — StoolSize chips, StoolColor swatches, urgency/strain selectors (1-5), blood toggle, note field; big Save button disabled until a type is chosen. Save calls repository create(); edit mode prefills from an existing BowelMovement and calls update(). Uses bowelMovementRepositoryProvider only. Widget tests for create + edit flows (drift-stream teardown rule applies).
acceptance_criteria: Sheet creates and edits entries end-to-end against in-memory DB in widget tests; type selection required; defaults to now; analyze clean

title: Timeline UI: today-at-a-glance header + grouped reverse-chron list + empty state
description: Replace HomeScreen placeholder: today-at-a-glance header (today's count, type icons) + reverse-chronological entry list grouped by day with date headers; entry tile shows Bristol icon, time (or 'all day' when dateOnly), detail badges, note preview. Friendly empty state for first run. Consumes dp-0ms.1 providers only — no direct DB access. Widget tests MUST end with pumpWidget(SizedBox.shrink()) + pump(Duration(milliseconds:1)) because live drift watch() streams wedge flutter_tester otherwise (see CLAUDE.md Test-run rules).
acceptance_criteria: Home shows header + grouped timeline against fixture data; empty state renders; widget tests green with drift-stream teardown; analyze clean

title: Home data providers: today summary + timeline stream + day grouping
description: Riverpod providers (riverpod_generator) in lib/features/home/ bridging BowelMovementRepository to the UI: (1) todaySummaryProvider — count + type breakdown for today via dailyTypeCounts/watchRange; (2) timelineProvider — stream of recent entries via watchRange (last 30 days), newest first; (3) pure day-grouping helper mapping List<BowelMovement> to date-keyed groups using LOCAL calendar days (occurredAt is local wall time by design). Unit tests with in-memory AppDatabase. No UI in this issue.
acceptance_criteria: Providers + grouping helper implemented with unit tests green; flutter analyze clean; no UI changes

title: Phase 1 completion: verify exit criteria, handoff doc, close epic

title: Web WASM smoke test + CI step

Replaces flutter test --platform chrome approach (unusable: dp-0ot). Also includes tool/setup_web.dart (version-locked wasm download + worker compile from source) and web/drift_worker.dart.
acceptance_criteria: DB_SMOKE OK line observed on chrome locally; flutter analyze clean; native suite green; flutter build web exits 0

title: Aggregation queries + tests (counts/day-week-month, avg/day, streaks/gaps)

Tests with hand-computed expected values incl. boundary days, empty ranges, dateOnly events counting toward daily stats, soft-deleted exclusion.

title: Fixture generator modeled on spreadsheet distributions

Synthetic only, never derived from the gitignored xlsx. Lives in lib/ so a later debug-seed feature can reuse it.
acceptance_criteria: determinism test (same seed = same output); totals within tolerance of avg/day; all events within range

Full gates: flutter analyze; flutter test --timeout 30s; flutter build web --release --base-href /DejaPoo/ (PowerShell, not Git Bash); flutter build apk --release; DB_SMOKE chrome probe; real-account manual sync check chrome+Android. Update README (Phase 5 Done, sync/export features, OAuth setup pointer), write designs/PHASE_5_HANDOFF.md, delete PHASE_5_CURRENT_STATUS.md, file deferred issues, close children + epic, push.

title: Export UI: Settings Export section (share sheet / download / Drive folder)
description: Settings Export section beside _ImportSection (same ConsumerStatefulWidget pattern): format choice XLSX/CSV. Delivery: mobile -> share_plus share sheet; web -> browser download (bytes, no dart:html deprecated APIs — use package:web); optionally upload to user-chosen Drive folder when signed in (drive.file scope, Drive picker can be minimal: fixed 'DejaPoo Exports' folder created on demand). Wire dp-l4h.3 generators.
acceptance_criteria: Manual gate: export XLSX on chrome + Android, re-import the exported file through Settings Import -> 0 inserted (idempotent).

title: SyncService: pull-merge-apply-push, triggers, status states, Sync now UI
description: SyncService: single-flight guard; cycle = read remote snapshot -> merge with local getAllIncludingDeleted via dp-l4h.1 engine -> applyRemote changed records -> write merged snapshot back (retry once on conflict by re-pulling). Triggers: app open (if signed in + authorized), debounced after entry save/edit/delete (~5s), manual Sync now button in Settings. Riverpod sync-status provider (idle/syncing/success(lastSyncAt)/error(msg)) driving the Settings sync row. Never blocks logging UX; failures are quiet + surfaced in Settings.
acceptance_criteria: Fake-store tests: two simulated devices with concurrent edits + deletes converge to identical DB state (DESIGN.md exit criterion); conflict retry works; sync errors never crash the app.

title: Drive appDataFolder snapshot store: interface, real impl, in-memory fake
description: Abstract DriveSnapshotStore interface: readSnapshot() -> json?/etag, writeSnapshot(json, {ifMatch}) with lost-update protection (Drive files.update + If-Match etag or generation check; document what Drive v3 actually supports and pick the strongest available guard). Real impl over googleapis drive v3 against appDataFolder (single file dejapoo_snapshot.json, create-if-absent). InMemoryDriveSnapshotStore fake with injectable conflict/failure modes for tests. No UI.
acceptance_criteria: Fake-backed unit tests cover create-if-absent, read-modify-write, conflict path, network-error surfacing as typed exceptions.

title: Google sign-in: auth provider, Settings account section, OAuth setup docs
description: Add google_sign_in (7.x — event-based authenticate API, web uses GIS button/renderButton, scopes need explicit authorization step), extension_google_sign_in_as_googleapis_auth, googleapis. Resolve versions carefully: drift/drift_dev MUST stay pinned 2.34.0; check analyzer transitive conflicts before committing pubspec. Riverpod auth-state provider (signed out / signed in / authorized for drive.appdata scope). Settings gains Account section (sign in/out, account email). App must degrade gracefully with no OAuth client configured (feature hidden or explains setup). Write designs/GOOGLE_OAUTH_SETUP.md: Cloud project, enable Drive API, Android client (SHA-1 debug+release), web client ID + how it is injected (index.html meta), iOS client (documented but unverifiable on Windows dev machine — file follow-up issue).
acceptance_criteria: flutter analyze clean; app builds web+android with and without OAuth config; sign-in flow works on chrome and Android emulator with a real test account (manual gate).

title: Export generators: XLSX year-sheet + CSV, importer round-trip
description: Generate XLSX matching the original spreadsheet layout (one sheet per year, date rows, Type 1-7 count columns C-I, total column J) and CSV equivalent, from all non-deleted events (timed events collapse to daily counts). Uses excel + csv packages already in deps. Round-trip test: export fixture data -> parse with Phase 4 xlsx_parser/csv_parser -> re-import via insertAllIfAbsent -> identical daily counts, 0 new inserts when re-importing over source data.
acceptance_criteria: Exported XLSX and CSV round-trip through the Phase 4 importer with exact count equality (DESIGN.md Phase 5 exit criterion).
close_reason: XLSX/CSV export generators implemented with round-trip test; all pass

title: Repository sync surface: getAllIncludingDeleted, applyRemote upsert, sync-state storage
description: Read .claude/skills/drift-flutter/SKILL.md FIRST. Add repository methods: getAllIncludingDeleted() (tombstones included) and applyRemote(List<BowelMovement>) — an upsert that writes records verbatim (does NOT bump updatedAt, CAN overwrite/resurrect soft-deleted rows, unlike insertAllIfAbsent). Add sync_state key-value drift table (schemaVersion bump + migration) for lastSyncAt/lastSnapshotHash. updatedAt/createdAt comparisons must be UTC-consistent — verify column converters before writing merge-adjacent code.
acceptance_criteria: Repo tests cover verbatim upsert, tombstone resurrection/overwrite, migration from current schema, sync_state read/write.

title: Sync model: snapshot JSON schema + LWW merge engine
description: Versioned JSON snapshot format (version, generatedAt, records[]) with toJson/fromJson for BowelMovement including tombstones; pure-Dart last-write-wins merge on updatedAt (newer record wins, tombstone wins ties toward deletion). No new dependencies. Unit tests: field round-trip, tombstone precedence, clock-skew ties, unknown-future-version rejection.
acceptance_criteria: Merge function is pure and fully unit-tested including two-way convergence property (merge(a,b)==merge(b,a) as sets).

title: H: Phase 4 wrap-up
description: README phase table + feature list. PHASE_4_HANDOFF.md from template. Delete status doc. Close children + epic. Full Session Completion checklist.

Real-file gate ran locally and matched exact expected totals 669/791/418 (2024/2025/2026 YTD), insertedCount 1878, fully idempotent on re-import. Full suite: 209 tests pass, flutter analyze clean.

Probe existing ids (chunked, <=500/chunk, no deletedAt filter), batch insert with InsertMode.insertOrIgnore, return inserted count. Tests: inserted count, dup skipped, soft-deleted not resurrected, >500 chunking.

title: Phase 3 completion: verification gate, README, handoff, PR
description: Orchestrator-run closeout (NOT a subagent). Full verification gate: flutter analyze clean; flutter test --timeout 30s all green; flutter run -d chrome --dart-define=DB_SMOKE=true prints DB_SMOKE OK then manual Reports spot-check (switch every range kind, log an entry and confirm the open Reports screen updates live), kill process stack per skill cleanup rules; flutter run -d android spot-check; iOS project still compiles (no Mac gate); flutter build web --release --base-href /DejaPoo/ exits 0 from PowerShell; if dp-mih landed, verify the deployed Pages build selects OPFS not IndexedDB. Then: update README.md (phase table + features), write designs/PHASE_3_HANDOFF.md from template, delete PHASE_3_CURRENT_STATUS.md, bd close children + epic dp-85t, open PR from phase_3 to main, run Session Completion checklist.
acceptance_criteria: All gate items pass with evidence recorded in PHASE_3_HANDOFF.md; README reflects Phase 3; PR open/merged; epic and children closed; git status up to date with origin.

title: Fixture-verified correctness gate (Phase 3 exit criterion)
description: The phase exit criterion: every range renders correct numbers against fixture data, verified by tests. Seed in-memory DB with deterministic known-answer data (hand-crafted, and/or lib/data/fixtures/fixture_generator.dart with a fixed seed) and assert for EVERY range kind (Day, Week, Month, Year, Custom) that the rendered stat tile values and chart bucket totals match independently computed expected numbers. Boundary cases: week straddling a month boundary, 23:59 local-time event stays on its local calendar day, empty range, dateOnly events counted in daily stats, most-common-type tie handling, pct-healthy rounding.
acceptance_criteria: A test file (e.g. test/features/reports/reports_correctness_test.dart) covers all five range kinds with exact expected numbers and the listed boundary cases; full suite green via flutter test --timeout 30s.

title: List tab with type filters
description: Filterable entry list for the selected range on the Reports screen List tab (scaffold owned by dp-85t.3 — this issue owns only the new list-tab widget file(s) + tests). Reverse-chron entries for the selected ReportRange, reusing/adapting the timeline_entry_tile.dart display pattern from home; multi-select Bristol-type filter chips (with BristolIcon); count of shown vs total; empty state when no matches. dateOnly entries display as all-day (no time), consistent with home.
acceptance_criteria: Widget tests: list shows only entries within the selected range; toggling filter chips narrows/restores the list; empty state renders. Drift stream teardown pump at end of every widget test. flutter analyze clean.

title: Reports screen assembly (range selector, tabs, Summary tab)
description: Replace the stub lib/features/reports/reports_screen.dart. Top: range selector bar — segmented control Day/Week/Month/Year/Custom, prev/next arrows stepping the anchor, period label; Custom opens showDateRangePicker. Below: Summary | List TabBar (List tab body is a placeholder owned by dp-85t.4). Summary tab: stat tile row (total, avg/day, most common type, pct healthy 3-5, longest gap; TodayHeader is the visual precedent, IconSizes.statTileIcon exists) + charts wired to dp-85t.1 providers and dp-85t.2 widgets. Bucketing: Week/Month = day bars; Year = month bars via rollUpByMonth; Day = tiles + donut only (no bar chart); Custom = day bars if span <= 62 days else month bars. Loading/empty states. Files: reports_screen.dart, lib/features/reports/widgets/ (selector, stat tiles).
acceptance_criteria: Widget tests: selector switches period kind and steps prev/next updating the label; Summary tab shows tiles and correct chart variant per range kind; custom picker round-trips a range. Drift stream teardown pump at end of every widget test. flutter analyze clean.

title: fl_chart + Bristol palette + chart widgets
description: Add fl_chart to pubspec.yaml (FIRST check the drift/riverpod analyzer version-pin trap in PHASE_1_HANDOFF.md / bd memory; fl_chart should be safe but verify pub solve). Add a 7-color per-Bristol-type chart palette to theme tokens (lib/ui/theme/), distinguishable in both light and dark schemes. Build pure presentational chart widgets in lib/features/reports/widgets/charts/: StackedTypeBarChart (takes pre-bucketed per-period type counts — day or month buckets — renders stacked bars colored by type) and TypeDonutChart (takes Map<BristolType,int>), each with a legend using BristolType.label and BristolIcon. No DB or providers — widgets take plain data so they are testable with static fixtures. Semantics labels on legend entries.
acceptance_criteria: Widget tests with static data verify bar stacking/heights and donut sections match input counts; empty-data states render without crashing; flutter analyze clean; flutter pub get resolves without breaking the drift 2.34.0 / riverpod pins.

title: Range model + reports providers
description: ReportRange value type: period kind (day/week/month/year/custom) + anchor date or custom bounds, deriving inclusive firstDay/lastDay, prev/next stepping, and a display label (e.g. "July 2026"). Week = Mon-Sun via existing weekStart() in lib/domain/aggregates.dart. Riverpod codegen providers in lib/features/reports/providers/: selected-range state + Future providers wrapping repository aggregations (dailyTypeCounts, typeDistribution, totalCount, averagePerDay, longestGapDays) plus derived ReportStats (total, avg/day, most common type, pct healthy types 3-5, longest gap). Providers must refresh when entries change (watch the ranges watchRange stream to invalidate; note watchRange is half-open [from,to) vs inclusive aggregation days). Files: lib/domain/report_range.dart, lib/features/reports/providers/*. Mirror lib/features/home/providers/timeline_providers.dart patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover firstDay/lastDay + stepping for every period kind incl. month-length and year boundaries; provider tests against in-memory DB verify stats values and that an insert refreshes results; flutter analyze clean.

title: GitHub Pages web deploy: COI service worker so drift gets OPFS (avoid permanent IndexedDB fallback)
description: GitHub Pages cannot send COOP/COEP headers, so the app is not cross-origin isolated and drift falls back to IndexedDB storage. Adopt BinderManager's pattern: register coi_serviceworker.js in web/index.html with a one-time reload BEFORE the app boots. Critical trap (documented in BinderManager index.html): if the app boots before the reload, drift creates the database in IndexedDB and keeps using it forever, even once OPFS becomes available. Must land before real usage of the Pages deployment (Phase 6 at the latest, ideally with Phase 2 when the app first becomes usable). See scratchpad clone or github.com/ryan-shah/BinderManager web/index.html + web/coi_serviceworker.js.

title: Phase 5: Google Drive sync & export
description: Google sign-in; sync via JSON snapshot in Drive appDataFolder (per-record last-write-wins on updatedAt, tombstones for deletes); manual Sync now + auto-sync on app open/entry save; CSV/XLSX export (matching original spreadsheet layout) to Drive folder or share sheet. Needs Google Cloud OAuth client per platform (user-side setup). See designs/DESIGN.md Phase 5.
acceptance_criteria: Two devices converge after concurrent edits; exported XLSX round-trips through the Phase 4 importer.

title: Phase 4: Historical spreadsheet import
description: Importer for the existing year-sheet layout (XLSX and CSV): date rows + Type 1-7 count columns, expands counts into dateOnly events, marks them imported, dedupes on re-import. Tests use a synthetic fixture mimicking the layout (real xlsx is gitignored, used for local manual check). See designs/DESIGN.md Phase 4.
acceptance_criteria: Real Alex Bowels.xlsx imports cleanly locally; Reports reproduce the spreadsheet year totals (669/791/418) and avg-per-day figures.
close_reason: Phase 4 wrap-up complete: README updated, handoff written, stale-row backlog filed (dp-h5e)

title: Phase 3: Metrics & reports
description: Reports screen with Day/Week/Month/Year/Custom range selector. Stat tiles (total, avg/day, most common type, pct in healthy range 3-5, longest gap), stacked bar chart of counts by type over time, type-distribution donut, filterable List view. Computed via Phase 1 aggregation queries. See designs/DESIGN.md Phase 3.
acceptance_criteria: Every range renders correct numbers against fixture data, verified by tests.

title: Re-add riverpod_lint + custom_lint when analyzer versions align
description: Dropped during dp-2ri.2: riverpod_generator 4.x (analyzer ^12) and drift_dev 2.34.x (analyzer ^12/^13) are incompatible with current custom_lint/riverpod_lint releases. Re-add both dev deps once their analyzer constraints align with the generator stack. analysis_options.yaml never referenced the plugin, so nothing else changed.

title: Phase 6: Polish & release readiness
description: Optional daily reminder notifications, app icon/splash, responsive wide-screen web layout + PWA manifest, accessibility pass (semantics on icon picker), empty/error/loading states, release builds, store listing prep. See designs/DESIGN.md Phase 6.
acceptance_criteria: Installable release artifacts for all three platforms.

title: Import stale-row cleanup: handle decreased counts on re-import
description: When a spreadsheet count decreases between re-imports, the old higher-count events remain as stale rows. insertAllIfAbsent only skips duplicates — it cannot detect or remove events whose source count dropped. Accepted limitation from Phase 4 design. Fix would require tracking import provenance and diffing.

title: Local 'flutter test --platform chrome' wedges on Windows dev machine
description: Three independent detached runs froze with identical signature: frontend_server (dartaotruntime) stops at ~31 CPUsec / ~680MB and never proceeds past 'loading test/web/db_smoke_test.dart'; one run got past compile in 24s then hung at 'Running test suite' (pre-asset-fix). Suspects: Windows Defender scanning, DDC test pipeline on Windows, or asset-manifest rebuild interaction. Workaround in place: web smoke gate verified in CI (ubuntu); CLAUDE.md Test-run rules document the wedge + orphan cleanup procedure. Investigate when it matters (e.g., pre-release local verification).
notes: RESOLVED-BY-WORKAROUND. Findings: (1) locally, even a trivial @TestOn('browser') test wedges nondeterministically — frontend_server freezes ~28-31 CPUsec at 'loading', so the wedge is toolchain-level, not drift/wasm/test-content; one run in ~6 reached the browser. (2) On CI ubuntu the pipeline works but the suite times out because the browser test harness does not serve the asset bundle (rootBundle.load never completes). Gate replaced by runtime DB_SMOKE probe (lib/data/db/db_smoke_probe.dart, flutter run -d chrome --dart-define=DB_SMOKE=true). Keep open only as a pointer if flutter tooling improves.
